title: NEO 3.1-C RT4-R1 UNLOCK_RECORD (authorized invariant-preserving edit of a locked path)
description: The per-slice REQUEST that asks AS4 (verify_app_slice.ps1) to return PASS-WITH-AUTH for a touch of a denylisted/locked path. The record is NOT the authority: AS4 grants PASS-WITH-AUTH only when this record BINDS to a recorded HUMAN gate in the host-held human-gate ledger (neo:human_gate_ledger) AND every per-path bind holds (artifact class == S3 class map, expected_post_sha == the on-disk re-pin, expected_prior_sha == the denylist recorded_sha when present) AND the named invariant behavioral test PASSED. Any failure keeps AS4 on the FAIL path (fail-closed). PROVISIONAL-DEV: in dev the ledger is not yet under a host-held root-of-trust (section 4.9 / S5) + Option A ACL (S6), so a builder CAN fabricate a ledger entry; the binding is tamper-EVIDENT, not tamper-PROOF. binding.root_of_trust MUST say so and binding.prod_gate MUST assert that RT4-R1 PASS-WITH-AUTH is not trusted in PROD until S5 anchors the ledger. Placed at SliceDir\UNLOCK_RECORD.json.

gate_ref.description: Id of the RECORDED HUMAN gate this unlock is bound to. Convention (OQ2): keyed to a roadmap section 8 decision-log id, e.g. '2026-06-30-P1-S3-END-KEEP'. MUST resolve to an entry in the human-gate ledger; an unresolved gate_ref is the self-unlock backdoor and FAILS.

authorized_by.description: The HUMAN principal named on the recorded gate (e.g. 'Raphael'). A role/agent value (NEO_BUILDER/DIRECTOR/AUDITOR/builder/...) is a self-issue and yields NO PASS. Must equal the ledger entry's authorized_by.

issued_by_gate.description: Which human gate discharged this. Must equal the ledger entry's gate_kind. 'builder'/'role' is not a valid value (fail-closed).

app_slug.description: Must equal the app profile identity.app_slug. Prevents a fixture/self-test authorization from authorizing a different app.

invariant_test_id.description: Id of the behavioral test proving the locked file's invariant survived the additive edit. Must map to a PASSING command_evidence entry (status=pass, exit_code=0). Interlocks with the AS15 invariant_preserved custom-check mode.

authorizations.description: One entry per authorized locked path. Every denylisted path touched by the slice MUST be covered here AND named in the ledger entry's authorized_paths, else AS4 FAILs (scope guard).

path.description: Repo-relative path of the locked file being edited.

artifact_class.description: Declared S3 artifact class; MUST equal what .neo/schema/artifact_classes.json resolves the path to (diff-scoped-to-a-declared-class bound).

expected_post_sha.description: Lowercase 64-hex SHA-256 of the file AFTER the invariant-preserving edit (the re-pin), or the literal 'DELETED' for an authorized deletion. MUST equal the on-disk SHA at verify time.

expected_prior_sha.description: Optional. Lowercase 64-hex SHA-256 of the LOCKED (pre-edit) file. When the denylist entry carries a recorded_sha (OQ1), this is REQUIRED and MUST equal it, proving we unlock the exact locked version.

root_of_trust.description: 'provisional-dev' = the ledger is a plain repo file the session principal can write (tamper-evident only) -> DEV ONLY. 'host-anchored' = the ledger is under a host-held root-of-trust outside the principal's write scope (section 4.9/S5 + Option A/S6). NEVER claim 'host-anchored' in dev.

prod_gate.description: Assertion that RT4-R1 PASS-WITH-AUTH is NOT trusted in PROD until section 4.9 (S5) anchors the ledger under a host-held root-of-trust + Option A (S6) ACL; the DEV->PROD push gate is the backstop.
